code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test11-NOV-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or acute kidney injury with glycosuria and ketonuria, but no evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
